title: Inconsistent Asset Handling - ERC20 Vault Accepts ETH Instead of ERC20 Tokens
reason: `IVault.deposit` is explicitly `payable`, and the implementation consistently uses ETH transfers on deposit. The `asset` variable appears to be metadata/accounting context, not proof that ERC20 transfers were intended here.

title: No Validation of Controller Implementation - Rogue Owner Can Steal All Funds
reason: This is owner-controlled configuration risk rather than a distinct vulnerability. A trusted owner can already redirect protocol behavior by choosing the controller.

title: Insufficient Validation of Withdraw Amount
reason: No concrete exploit is established from vault code alone. The controller API intentionally returns `(withdrawn, fee)`, so `withdrawn < assets` can be part of expected controller semantics rather than a vault bug.

title: Division by Zero in Share Calculation When Total Assets Returns Zero
reason: This requires an already broken or inconsistent controller state (`totalSupply() > 0` while `totalAssets() == 0`). It is not an independently exploitable protocol bug from the vault logic itself.

title: No Slippage Protection in Deposit Function
reason: Generic lack of a `minShares` parameter is not reportable by itself here. The concrete harmful pricing bugs are already captured in separate findings.

title: Missing Input Validation in convertToShares and convertToAssets
reason: Potential view-function reverts under pathological controller states do not create meaningful standalone protocol harm.

title: Missing Zero Address Check for SubStrategy
reason: `setSubStrategy` already rejects the zero address. Lack of contract-code validation is an admin-trust concern, not a separate exploit.

title: Console.log Statements Left in Production Code
reason: Gas/debug residue is not a security finding with realistic protocol-level harm.

title: Inconsistent Return Value in Withdraw Function
reason: This is an integration ergonomics issue, not a vulnerability affecting funds or protocol safety.

title: Controller Can Be Set to Address(0) After Initialization
reason: `setController` forbids the zero address. An unset controller after initialization is deployment misconfiguration, not an exploitable vulnerability.
